name: Pika API
what_it_does: Pika is a consumer-focused AI video generation platform with a beta API for text-to-video and image-to-video creation.
use_cases: Generating short creative video clips from text prompts
Animating images into short video sequences
Prototyping AI video features for consumer-facing products
Exploring AI video generation capabilities in early-access integrations
not_for: Production workloads requiring stable, well-documented APIs
Applications needing SLA guarantees or rate limit clarity
Developers who need comprehensive SDK support or error handling guidance
best_when: Best for early adopters and experimenters who want to prototype with AI video generation and are comfortable with a beta, consumer-first API that may change.
avoid_when: Avoid for any production or enterprise use case — the API is early-stage, documentation is sparse, and stability guarantees are limited.

auth.notes: API key authentication; access granted via early-access program, not publicly open

pricing.free_tier_limits: Limited free generations on platform; API access may require separate arrangement
pricing.paid_tiers: Subscription plans on consumer platform; API pricing not publicly documented
pricing.notes: API pricing structure is opaque — primarily a consumer product with limited API monetization clarity

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; effectively limited by early-access tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation and error messages are minimal; agents must infer behavior from sparse examples
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support; each request is a new generation
known_agent_gotchas: API is early-access/beta — endpoints and behavior can change without notice
No official SDK means agents must handle raw HTTP with minimal guardrails
Async generation requires polling; no webhook support documented
Rate limits and quotas are undocumented — unexpected throttling is likely
Content moderation is aggressive and consumer-tuned, rejecting many creative prompts
API access may be revoked or limited without warning during beta period

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced; API key auth; no scope granularity; limited security documentation
